title: Doryopteris crenulans
taxonomy: Doryopteris crenulans is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Doryopteris and the order Pteridales. Within the plant kingdom, Doryopteris crenulans is classified as a vascular plant, specifically a type of pteridophyte. The kingdom classification for Doryopteris crenulans is Plantae, and it is further classified as a member of the division Pteridophyta. At the rank of species, Doryopteris crenulans is classified as Doryopteris crenulans (Mett. ex Kuhn) C. Chr.
uses: Doryopteris crenulans, also known as the rough shield fern, has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its medicinal properties. Its extracts have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies are necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.

intro: Doryopteris crenulans, also known as the palm fern or West Indian palm fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Pteridaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Doryopteris crenulans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as to boost the immune system.
